title: BTS Plays First Concert Together in Four Years
summary: BTS, a famous K-pop band from South Korea, performed their first concert together in nearly four years. The event took place at Gwanghwamun Square in Seoul, South Korea, on a Saturday. About 260,000 fans came to see the show, but only 22,000 people with free tickets could enter the concert area. Others watched on big screens outside. The band played songs from their new album called Arirang, which was released on Friday. BTS has seven members: RM, Jin, Suga, J-Hope, Jimin, V, and Jung Kook. Fans from around the world were excited because they missed seeing the band perform together. The concert was also streamed live on Netflix so more people could watch. This event is important for South Korea because BTS helps share Korean culture globally.

background_read: BTS is a K-pop band from South Korea that started in 2013. They have become very popular worldwide, with millions of fans called the BTS Army. K-pop stands for Korean pop music, which often includes singing, dancing, and colorful performances. BTS is known for their songs about self-love and social issues. They have won many awards and broken records. This concert was special because it was their first time performing together as a full group in almost four years, as some members were doing solo projects or military service. South Korea uses BTS to share its culture, like music and language, with other countries, which is called soft power.
Article_Structure: WHO: BTS, a K-pop band with seven members, and their fans from around the world.
WHAT: A concert where BTS performed together for the first time in nearly four years, featuring songs from their new album Arirang.
WHERE: Gwanghwamun Square in Seoul, South Korea.
WHY: To celebrate their reunion and share Korean culture globally, bringing joy to fans after a long wait.

perspectives.description: Fans were very happy and emotional to see BTS perform together again after years of waiting.
The event shows how BTS helps South Korea share its culture with the world, making it more popular.
